summary: The diff implements most of the product variants requirements. Backend (REQ-1, REQ-2) adds a ProductVariant type and variants field to Product in main.mo. Frontend (REQ-3) adds inline variant editing in StoreProductManager. Frontend (REQ-4) adds variant selector to ProductCard. Frontend (REQ-5) adds ProductVariant type and extends Product in types/index.ts. Frontend (REQ-6) adds variant mutation hooks in useQueries.ts. However, several specific acceptance criteria have gaps: the ProductVariant type uses 'stockAdjustment' instead of the spec's 'stock' (optional Nat); priceAdjustment is Nat (not optional/signed) in backend; add-to-cart variant info passthrough is only partially implemented.

missing_requirements.requirement: Each variant has: name, value, priceAdjustment (optional, positive or negative), and stock (optional Nat).
missing_requirements.reason: The implemented ProductVariant type uses 'stockAdjustment: Nat' (not optional 'stock: Nat') in both backend/main.mo and frontend/src/types/index.ts. priceAdjustment is typed as Nat in the backend, which prevents negative price adjustments as the spec requires.

missing_requirements.requirement: Add-to-cart includes the selected variant information.
missing_requirements.reason: In ProductCard.tsx, handleAddToCart calls addToCart.mutateAsync with only { productId, quantity } — no variant index or variant label is passed to the cart mutation. The variant is only used for a toast display label.

missing_requirements.requirement: ProductVariant type is defined with correct fields (name, value, priceAdjustment, stock).
missing_requirements.reason: The ProductVariant interface in types/index.ts uses 'stockAdjustment' instead of 'stock', and priceAdjustment is a required number rather than optional. The spec requires priceAdjustment to be optional and stock to be optional Nat.

hallucinated_features.description: A backend/migration.mo module was added to handle data migration of existing products to include the new variants field.
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request did not request a migration module. While pragmatically useful, it was not specified as a requirement.

hallucinated_features.description: ProductVariant uses a 'stockAdjustment' field (delta-based stock offset) instead of the spec's 'stock' (optional absolute stock quantity per variant).
hallucinated_features.reason: The spec defines stock as 'optional Nat' representing variant-specific stock quantity, not a delta adjustment on base product stock. The implementation uses stockAdjustment as an additive offset applied to product.stock, which is a different semantic model not requested.
